sections.label: 1-25. sorularda, cümlede boş bırakılan yere uygun düşen kelime ya da ifadeyi bulunuz.
26-33. sorularda, yarım bırakılan cümleyi uygun şekilde tamamlayan ifadeyi bulunuz.
52-59. sorularda, verilen Türkçe cümlenin İngilizce dengini bulunuz.
60-67. sorularda verilen cümlenin Türkçe dengini bulunuz.
68-73. sorularda, parçada boş bırakılan yeri uygun şekilde tamamlayan ifadeyi bulunuz.
74-80. sorularda, verilen durumda söylenebilecek sözü bulunuz.

passages.title: Native Americans
passages.text: Native Americans are often called "Indians". This is one result of Columbus's mistaken belief that he had landed in India. His successors passed down many other inaccurate descriptions of the Native Americans. They were described as savages, though it was whites who slaughtered hundreds of thousands of them. They were called lazy, although it was whites who forced them to give up their traditional occupations. These false ideas about Native Americans were strengthened by the ways whites described themselves. White settlers were known as "pioneers" though they were really invaders and thieves; when they stole the Native Americans' land, it was called "homesteading", not robbery.

passages.title: Tobacco
passages.text: Although there was scientific evidence linking smoking and lung cancer in the 1950s, acceptance was slow. Each decade brought more evidence and more forceful warnings. Now it is absolutely clear that tobacco is truly a "killer weed", and is a bigger public health threat than all other drugs combined, including alcohol. For example, though lung cancer is not a common disease, almost all lung cancers occur in smokers. Smoking is also estimated to be related to about 30% of all other types of cancer, to about 30% of deaths resulting from heart disease, and 80-90% of deaths from chronic obstructive lung disease.

passages.title: Miai
passages.text: In Japan there is a custom called miai. It is a meeting between a man and a woman on the understanding that they may choose to marry one another. This meeting is arranged by a "go-between", usually an older person who knows both partners. For example, a teacher may try to match a former student with a relative. First of all, photographs and family backgrounds are exchanged. After that, if they agree, the couple meet either at the go-between's house or at a restaurant. The go-between introduces them, then leaves them to talk by themselves. They might go to a movie or a coffee shop. Later they report to the go-between on whether they want to meet again. Ideally, they will agree to marry after several meetings.

passages.title: Tourism
passages.text: Tourism often has negative results for local communities. Tourists go to museums, buy souvenirs, enjoy the beaches and see the sights without interacting with the local people. For this reason, local people often see visitors only as a source of money. There is no interaction at all except over money, and so there is no mutual respect. When young people see visitors with plenty of cash they may grow unhappy with their traditional way of life. There may be environmental changes as homes and villages are often destroyed to make way for hotels. Although tourism brings in money, it leaves behind problems like discontented young people and environmental damage.

passages.title: Factual Material
passages.text: Readers of factual material should bear in mind this aspect: their authors, like authors of fiction, have beliefs and theories that affect the way they present their subject matter. For example, a British professor's account of the American Revolution would be different from the version written by a professor from the United States. Because of national loyalties, the two scholars might look at the events from different angles: the former as a colonial rebellion on a distant continent, and the second as a struggle for personal freedom. The two authors would write from different points of view and express certain opinions because they have different ways of looking at the subject.

passages.title: Medieval Christians
passages.text: Medieval Christians confronted Muslims chiefly in military crusades, in Spain and the Holy Land, and in theology. From this encounter came the restoration of ancient learning to the West. The Reconquista in Spain gradually pushed the Moors south from the Pyrenees, and among the treasures left behind were Arabic translations of Greek works of science and philosophy. In 1085 the city of Toledo, with one of the finest libraries in Islam, fell to the Christians. Among the occupiers were Christian monks who quickly began the process of translating ancient works into Latin. By the end of the 12th century, much of the ancient heritage was again available to the Latin West.

options.A: Yoga egzersizlerinin vücudun pek çok fonksiyonunu düzene koyduğunu Batılı doktorlar da kabul etmiştir.
options.B: Batılı doktorlar, yoga egzersizleri ile vücudun pek çok fonksiyonunun kontrol edilebileceğini kabul etmektedirler.
options.C: Batılı doktorlara göre, yoga egzersizleri ile vücudun fonksiyonlarını kontrol altına almak mümkündür.
options.D: Batılı doktorlar, yoga egzersizleri sonucu vücudun pek çok fonksiyonunun daha kontrollü olduğunu görmüşlerdir.
options.E: Yoga egzersizleri ile vücudun daha fonksiyonel bir duruma geldiğini Batılı doktorlar da savunmaktadır.

questions.question: Luxembourg owes its high standard of living, in part, to the fact that the labour force makes up almost half of its population.

options.A: Lüksemburg'da, nüfusun yarıdan fazlasını çalışan kesimin oluşturduğu bölgelerde hayat standardı son derece yüksektir.
options.B: Nüfusun neredeyse yarısını çalışan kesimin oluşturduğu Lüksemburg'da, özellikle bazı bölgeler yüksek yaşam standardına sahiptir.
options.C: Lüksemburg'da yaşam standardı çok yüksektir çünkü nüfusun hemen hemen yarısı çalışan kesimden oluşmaktadır.
options.D: Lüksemburg, yüksek yaşam standardını insanların çalışkanlığına borçlu olan nüfusun yarıdan fazlası çalışmaktadır.
options.E: Lüksemburg, yüksek yaşam standardını kısmen, nüfusunun neredeyse yarısını çalışan kesimin oluşturmasına borçludur.

questions.question: Before a city plan was adopted in 1889, Sao Paulo looked more like a capital city of colonial times.

options.A: Sao Paulo, sömürge döneminde bir başkenti andıran görünümünden 1889'da, şehrin yeniden planlanmasıyla kurtuldu.
options.B: 1889'a kadar sömürge döneminin başkenti olma özelliğini sürdüren Sao Paulo, o dönemde planlı şehirleşmeye başlamıştır.
options.C: 1889'da planlı gelişmeye başlamadan önce Sao Paulo, aynı sömürge döneminde bir başkent görünümündeydi.
options.D: 1889'da bir şehir planı benimsenmeden önce, Sao Paulo daha çok sömürge döneminin bir başkenti gibi görünüyordu.
options.E: Sömürge döneminde başkent olan Sao Paulo, 1889'da şehir planı çizilene dek çok düzensiz bir şekilde kentleşmişti.

questions.question: Performing experiments on animals to increase our medical knowledge is justifiable, but using them for cosmetic products is cruel.

options.A: Tıp bilgimizi arttırmak için hayvanlar üzerinde deney yapmak savunulabilir ancak onları kozmetik ürünleri için kullanmak zalimliktir.
options.B: Tıp ve kozmetik alanında bilgi edinmek için hayvanlar üzerinde deney yapılması hiçbir şekilde onaylanamayacak bir vahşettir.
options.C: Hayvanlar üzerinde deney yapmak tıp bilgimizi arttırması bakımından gereklidir ama kozmetik ürünleri için bu durum mazur görülemez.
options.D: Tıp ve kozmetik ürünleri hakkındaki bilgimizi arttırsa da, deneyler için hayvanları kullanmak vahşetten başka bir şey değildir.
options.E: İster tıp bilgimizi arttırmak ister kozmetik ürünleri için olsun, hayvanlar üzerinde deney yapmak zalimliktir.

questions.question: There are such a lot of daily activities we simply do through habit that we don't even need to think when performing them.

options.A: Bir gün içerisinde yapmak zorunda olduğumuz o kadar çok iş var ki bunların hepsini düşünerek yapmamız mümkün değil.
options.B: Günlük yaşamımızda sürekli yaptığımız, bu yüzden de alışkanlık halini almış pek çok işimizin olduğunu çoğu zaman düşünmeyiz bile.
options.C: Sadece alışkanlıktan dolayı yaptığımız o kadar çok günlük iş var ki bunları yaparken düşünmeye bile gerek duymayız.
options.D: Günlük yaşamda yaptığımız işlerin çoğu, düşünmemizi bile gerektirmeyen, basit, alışkanlık halini almış işlerdir.
options.E: Yapmak zorunda olduğumuz günlük işlerimizin çoğunu, alışkanlık halini aldıkları için, hiç düşünmeden yaparız.

questions.leading_text: Fear of the number 13 has long been a superstition. Its roots are religious. At the Last Supper of Jesus and his disciples, there were 13 persons, one of whom was Judas, the traitor. Today many tall buildings omit a numbered 13th floor, skipping instead from 12 to 14. Nevertheless, the _____ trick seems to be enough to reassure people that they are safe from bad luck.
questions.question: Parçada boş bırakılan yeri en uygun şekilde tamamlayan ifadeyi bulunuz.

options.A: Avoidance of black cats has religious origins as well
options.B: This does not make any difference for the blind, though
options.C: Seeing a black cat is also believed to bring bad luck
options.D: In such buildings, accidents and mishaps have been shown to be less frequent
options.E: This practice obviously can't really eliminate the thirteenth floor

questions.leading_text: Lightships are aids to navigation, similar in function to lighthouses. They came into use in the 17th century in places where it was not practical to build a lighthouse. Modern lightships are steel vessels about 35 metres long. _____ Some lightships, however, those equipped with automatic devices, require no full-time crew on board.
questions.question: Parçada boş bırakılan yeri en uygun şekilde tamamlayan ifadeyi bulunuz.

options.A: They are most suitable for sheltered waters where high-powered illumination is not necessary
options.B: Usually they are manned by a crew of about seven, and carry all the equipment standard to a lighthouse
options.C: Because lightships are often located in remote places, the power used to operate them is usually derived from diesel generators
options.D: To increase light intensity and focus it into a beam, mirrors and other reflectors came into use in the 18th century
options.E: Modern lighthouses have reflectors, as well as lenses and prisms, to carry the light farther

questions.leading_text: People of all ages in nearly every country where there is mountainous terrain enjoy the unique appeal of skiing. It is one of the few sports that enable people to move at high rates of speed without any power-producing device. With the world's top athletes reaching speeds over 80 mph in the downhill, it is no wonder serious injuries are common. _____
questions.question: Parçada boş bırakılan yeri en uygun şekilde tamamlayan ifadeyi bulunuz.

options.A: Yet female skiers are actually less liable to injury than males, although they get hurt occasionally
options.B: Actually, it is rather miraculous that fatal injuries are extremely rare
options.C: In its simplest form, skiing is sliding down a snow-covered slope on a pair of long, slim runners called skis
options.D: However, it can be a very dangerous sport, particularly at the professional level
options.E: Just recently American skier Peekabo Street suffered a broken leg during a competition

questions.leading_text: In recent years, 'supermarkets' and the even larger 'hypermarkets' have spread across the landscape. _____ Not everyone agrees, though, that they are a good thing. Those who object to these vast stores point to increased traffic caused not only by delivery trucks but also by cars travelling to them. They are also blamed for the destruction of local business and the resulting decline in town centres.
questions.question: Parçada boş bırakılan yeri en uygun şekilde tamamlayan ifadeyi bulunuz.

options.A: They are extremely inconvenient for people who do not have cars
options.B: As competition has become keener, supermarkets have added specialty shops, like small bakeries into their stores
options.C: Everything today seems to be called 'super-', so the word is losing any real meaning
options.D: A variation on the supermarket is the box store, which sells products directly out of cartons
options.E: They are popular because they provide a great variety of products, and are generally cheaper than traditional shops

questions.leading_text: In the bronze age, the time of the Trojan Wars, ships seldom ventured out of sight of land because they had large crews to man the oars, and little space for provisions or sleeping. Nor were they able to withstand heavy weather, and their captains did not know how to find their way without landmarks to guide them. Thus, the crew would cook, eat and sleep ashore. _____
questions.question: Parçada boş bırakılan yeri en uygun şekilde tamamlayan ifadeyi bulunuz.

options.A: The Polynesians were far ahead of the Europeans, being able to find their way guided by the stars
options.B: Occasionally a storm would take them far from land, and then, it was regarded as a disaster
options.C: For these reasons, they had to stop in a harbour very frequently
options.D: This means that they were often able to cover huge distances in a short time
options.E: However, because they had oars, they could still progress in light or strong winds

questions.leading_text: Typing is the most basic skill of the information-based economy. Those with typing skills, which can be acquired through a relatively basic course, are virtually assured of employment, though the pay may be low. _____ This, obviously, requires more extensive education.
questions.question: Parçada boş bırakılan yeri en uygun şekilde tamamlayan ifadeyi bulunuz.

options.A: The newest printing method in the industry is computerized, or electronic, printing
options.B: Combined with knowledge of computer programs, good typing skills can get one a high paying job
options.C: The electronic typewriter is similar in appearance to the manual machines
options.D: Typing can also be very useful in your personal life, helping with letters and reports
options.E: Yet many people may think that a low paying job is better than being unemployed

questions.leading_text: You are working in a clothing shop. It is the policy of the shop not to reduce the price of any items for any reason. A customer likes a shirt, but finds a small defect and asks to have it at a cheaper price. You say:
questions.question: Bu durumda ne dersiniz?

options.A: You're right, Sir. I wouldn't pay full price for that if I were you.
options.B: I think I need to have my eyes checked. I can't see any defects in this shirt!
options.C: I'm sorry, Sir. Although you have a point, we are not authorized to make any reductions.
options.D: I'm afraid that is the cheapest shirt we have in stock, Sir.
options.E: We don't have cheaper ones at the moment, but we're expecting a new order in two days.

questions.leading_text: You have been looking forward all week to watching the big match on TV. Five minutes before the match starts, your next door neighbour drops in, saying that her TV set has just broken down, and that the film she has always wanted to see is about to start. Determined to go ahead with your plan, you say to her:
questions.question: Bu durumda ne dersiniz?

options.A: Oh great, my wife has always wanted to watch that film too.
options.B: I'm sorry Mrs Green, but the football match hasn't finished yet, so you'll miss the beginning.
options.C: Why didn't you go and see it when it was showing at the cinema?
options.D: Can't you wait until 10 o'clock, when the football match is due to finish?
options.E: I'm afraid you'll miss the film, Mrs Green, as I'm watching a football match tonight.

questions.leading_text: You are attending an important meeting. The chairman asks everyone to switch off their mobile phones. Sure that yours is switched off, you don't bother to check, but, just as you are reaching a crucial point of the meeting, your phone rings. Embarrassed, you say:
questions.question: Bu durumda ne dersiniz?

options.A: I'm sorry, but I thought it was already turned off, and didn't check it.
options.B: I suddenly remembered that I had to phone my family to inform them about something.
options.C: I'm not very happy with my phone line and want to change it.
options.D: Isn't modern technology amazing? Now we can be reached wherever we are.
options.E: I know you asked us once nicely, but I have to answer it in case it is important.

questions.leading_text: You've done a lot of work on your balcony garden and are quite proud of it. You're talking to a friend on your balcony and are horrified to see him about to put his cigarette out in one of your pot plants. You don't want to seem too upset or unreasonable, but want to stop him before he does so. Almost hurriedly, you say:
questions.question: Bu durumda ne dersiniz?

options.A: Hey, here's an ashtray for you. I'd rather you used it than the pot.
options.B: How would you feel if I put my cigarette out on your new carpet?
options.C: Don't even think about it! That's no place to put out your cigarette.
options.D: What do you think you're doing? Does that look like an ashtray to you?
options.E: If you want to smoke, I would prefer that you did it in the parking lot.

questions.leading_text: Your boss invites you to a cocktail party at his house. The day of the party you learn that the guest of honour is going to be a politician you have always despised for being dishonest and self-seeking. You cannot stand the thought of being a guest at the same party as him, nor do you want to let your boss know the truth, so you make up an excuse:
questions.question: Bu durumda ne dersiniz?

options.A: This is a perfect opportunity for me to tell him what most people think about him.
options.B: I think I'm coming down with flu and I wouldn't want to give it to anyone else.
options.C: Not him! He's the most corrupt and dishonest man in the country.
options.D: Forgive me, but I wouldn't be in the same room with such a person.
options.E: Are there going to be any other politicians?

questions.leading_text: You're a small business owner and hire a worker who, after his one month probationary period, you decide to let go. He was a nice enough person, but his work just wasn't up to the standard. A week later, you get a phone call from another company asking for a reference. You don't want to hurt his chances at another job, but you don't feel right about recommending him too strongly either, so you say:
questions.question: Bu durumda ne dersiniz?

options.A: He's a really nice person, but I don't ever remember him doing a bit of work.
options.B: I'd hire him again in a second! Workers like him don't come along every day.
options.C: I'm afraid I must tell you the truth. I could only bear him for a month!
options.D: Well, he wasn't with us long enough for me to get too much of an impression.
options.E: If you're looking for a friend, he's the man. If it's a worker you want, he's not.

questions.leading_text: A friend comes to visit for a few days, so you tell him to make himself at home. He seems to be having some problems, so you let him stay for a few more days. Four weeks later, he's still at your flat, shows no intention of leaving and his presence is upsetting you and your flatmate. To get rid of him without hurting him too much, you say:
questions.question: Bu durumda ne dersiniz?

options.A: Don't you really hate it when people take advantage and abuse your hospitality?
options.B: You've got twenty-four hours to pack up and hit the road, or I'll throw you out.
options.C: If you intend to stay here longer, I think you've got to start paying us some rent.
options.D: I used to think of you as a friend, but I'm beginning to see you as a parasite.
options.E: Listen, this is very difficult for me, but I think you've been here long enough.
